originalBehavior: The opening matrix scene lets readers drag the two basis-column arrows and the sample vector while the transformed point, matrix equation, and Av vector all stay synchronized.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published IntroCtrl and BasisCtrl structure, including the draggable nobs, matrix-equation overlays, and transformed vector updates.
notes: The public route only wraps the wide visualization for narrow screens.
No controller logic was extracted or refactored for v1.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still renders the opening simple-plot scene with two matrix-column drags and one sample-vector drag.
Smoke coverage updates the local basis state and checks the plotted basis/vector geometry changes.

originalBehavior: The second plot shows eigenvector alignment, eigenvalue readouts, eigenspace guides, and repeated-multiplication behavior while the candidate vector moves.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published TransCtrl and PopulationCtrl views, including eigenvalue labels, eigenspace overlays, and multi-step trajectory updates.
notes: The published labels λ₁, λ₂, s₁, and s₂ still come from the original Angular watchers.
The local route preserves the original nested controller layout instead of splitting the plots apart.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still places the eigenvalue labels and repeated-sequence plot directly below the basis scene.
Smoke coverage checks that changing the candidate-vector state updates the visible geometry and eigen-alignment feedback.

originalBehavior: The amoeba section advances generation by generation, updates the simulation and side plot together, and shows the Fibonacci totals in the readout below.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published BacteriaCtrl, BacteriaPlotCtrl, bacteria-simulation, bacteria-plot, and fibonacci-sequence directives, including reset and forward controls.
notes: The local route keeps the original forward-step cadence and count text.
Only overflow wrappers were added around the two-up visual stage.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still ships reset and forward buttons below the bacteria simulation.
Smoke coverage advances the local generation counter and checks the simulation state and textual counts change.

originalBehavior: The migration section combines a draggable population/probability map with a hover-to-play steady-state animation and a synchronized stochastic-matrix multiplication panel.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published MigrationCtrl, sf-to-ny-migration-map, migration, and stochastic-matrix-multiplication surfaces, with one local path repair for the map JSON.
notes: The local copy loads the US map data from ./resources/us.json so the page works from a GitHub Pages subpath.
The original upstream cross-link to Setosa's Markov page is replaced with the local replica route.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still requests /ev/scripts/topojson.js and ../resources/us.json for the migration scene.
Smoke coverage waits for the local map SVG to render and checks the hover-driven steady-state animation produces sample history.

originalBehavior: The closing scene lets readers drag the matrix columns, adjust the number of steps, and watch the transformed point spiral while the complex eigenvalues update in the companion plane.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published FourQuadCtrl and four-quad-plot directive, including the step slider, complex-plane readout, and draggable basis vectors.
notes: The local route preserves the original step slider label and point-trail behavior.
No alternate controls were added for v1.
evidence: Live source verified 2026-03-14 still ends with a steps slider above the four-quad plot.
Smoke coverage changes the local basis state and confirms the complex-plane or spiral geometry updates.

originalBehavior: The published page depended on remote MathJax and surrounded the explainer with EV branding, creator links, share widgets, newsletter prompts, analytics, and comments.
localStatus: The local route loads MathJax from the shared local EV asset tree and uses the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: Technical provenance stays in docs only.
The body now routes back to the local replica index instead of /ev/.
evidence: The published page verified 2026-03-14 still points to cdnjs for MathJax and includes the old EV public chrome.
Public-surface auditing blocks that chrome from shipping on local routes.
